article_meta.title: US News exclusive ICE agent who opened fire on Renee Good was dragged, hospitalized by illegal migrant driver last year

transcript.content: Identify bias in this news article. Output ONLY a valid JSON array. No prose, no markdown fences. Format: [{"biasType": "...", "biasedText": "...", "explanation": "..."}] Return [] if no bias found.

Analyze this article:

---
HEADLINE: US News exclusive ICE agent who opened fire on Renee Good was dragged, hospitalized by illegal migrant driver last year 
SOURCE: New York Post (News)

ARTICLE:

US News exclusive ICE agent who opened fire on Renee Good was dragged, hospitalized by illegal migrant driver last year By Chris Nesi and Priscilla DeGregory Published Jan. 8, 2026 Updated Jan. 8, 2026, 6:52 p.m. ET The ICE agent who opened fire in Minneapolis Wednesday was dragged 100 yards by an illegal migrant in Minnesota last June after his arm was trapped inside the vehicle during a traffic stop, The Post can reveal. The attack on the officer, identified in court documents as Jonathan Ross, happened June 17 in Bloomington, Minnesota, exactly a month after embattled Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz labeled ICE agents "modern-day Gestapo" while speaking at a University of Minnesota Law School graduation in May. ICE agents conducted a traffic stop on Roberto Carlos Munoz, a serial illegal immigrant from Guatemala with a lengthy rap sheet with charges including domestic assault and sex crimes against an underage teenager, according to records. Munoz refused to exit his vehicle when officers approached his car, and Ross broke the back driver's side window in order to open the vehicle from the inside. 5 Roberto Carlos Munoz refused to exit his vehicle when he was approached by ICE agents in June. Department of Homeland Security 5 The aftermath of the shooting that left Renee Nicole Good dead. CRAIG LASSIG/EPA/Shutterstock The suspect then sped away with Ross' arm caught between the seat and the car frame, according to the Justice Department. Prosecutors said he was violently dragged more than 100 yards as the suspect weaved back and forth in an attempt to shake him loose from the car. Ross was hospitalized with "significant injuries to his arm and hand," requiring 33 stitches, but made a full recovery, DHS Assistant Secretary Tricia McLaughlin said in a statement at the time. Explore More Billionaire Bill Ackman donates $10K to GoFundMe for ICE agent who fatally shot Renee Nicole Good Minneapolis schools betray kids to feed ICE hysteria NYC anti-ICE protest draws 2,000 in latest unrest since shooting death of Renee Nicole Good A photo shared by CBS Minnesota showed the agent laid out on a hospital bed displaying his arms, one covered in blood seeping from several deep gashes. Munoz was arrested and charged with assaulting the officer and was convicted by a federal jury last month. A defense attorney for Munoz didn't immediately return a request for comment Thursday. 5 A photo shared by CBS Minnesota showed ICE agent Jonathan Ross laid out on a hospital bed displaying his arms, one covered in blood from deep gashes. 5 An officer is seen chasing a vehicle with Renee Nicole Good inside. X/@maxnesterak 5 The ICE agent, identified as Jonathan Ross, who was allegedly involved in Wednesday's deadly shooting. X/@maxnesterak That assault is part of a sky-rocketing trend of car attacks on ICE agents — with such incidents surging 3200% in the last year, according to stats released by DHS Thursday. Agents reported 66 attacks compared to only two during the same period in 2024, the data showed. Federal immigration officers have also reported a 1,347% increase in assaults and a whopping 8,000% surge in death threats during the first year of President Trump's second term. Ross fired three shots — killing Renee Nicole Good, 37 — on Wednesday after she drove her car toward him during an altercation on the street. DHS Secretary Kristi Noem said the "experienced" officer, a 10-year veteran of the agency, had acted in self defense and was justified in opening fire, and that Good was part of a "mob of agitators" attempting to thwart ICE's efforts in enforcing President Trump's immigration crackdown. Ross, who based in Minneapolis full time, is a member of the ICE ERO Special Response Team, a specially-trained tactical unit, law enforcement sources told The Post. Wide-angle footage of the incident appears to show that Good clipped Ross with her car after ignoring orders to exit the vehicle. Start your day with all you need to know Morning Report delivers the latest news, videos, photos and more. Thanks for signing up! Enter your email address Please provide a valid email. By clicking above you agree to the Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. Never miss a story. Check out more newsletters He quickly jumped out of the way, firing a single shot through the windshield and two additional shots through the open driver's side window, striking her in the head. President Trump echoed Noem's defense of the agent, telling the New York Times he acted in self defense after being struck by Good's car. The shooting poured gasoline on the long-smoldering tension between federal immigration agents and protesters who have fought back — at times violently — against the presence of agencies like ICE enforcing the law in their communities. OB_elements : []; var OB_element = {}; // Editable section. OB_element.containerId = "outbrainContainer0"; OB_element.widgetId = ob_id; OB_elements.push( OB_element );

parsed_output.explanation: The term 'illegal migrant' is politically charged language. More neutral alternatives would be 'undocumented immigrant' or 'person without legal immigration status.' The phrase appears designed to evoke negative emotional responses rather than objectively describe the person's legal status.

The descriptor 'embattled' is an editorial characterization that frames Gov. Walz negatively without providing context or justification. It's an opinion presented as fact within supposedly objective reporting.

parsed_output.biasedText: exactly a month after embattled Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz labeled ICE agents 'modern-day Gestapo'
parsed_output.explanation: This quote is presented to suggest causation or relevance to the agent's assault, creating an implicit narrative that criticism of ICE leads to violence against agents. The temporal connection doesn't establish causal connection, and including this appears designed to discredit ICE critics.

parsed_output.biasedText: serial illegal immigrant from Guatemala with a lengthy rap sheet
parsed_output.explanation: The phrase 'serial illegal immigrant' uses inflammatory language. The term 'serial' typically refers to serial criminals, and its use here appears designed to dehumanize. 'Rap sheet' is also colloquial rather than neutral terminology.

parsed_output.biasedText: Good was part of a 'mob of agitators' attempting to thwart ICE's efforts
parsed_output.explanation: Presenting DHS Secretary's characterization as 'mob of agitators' without balancing perspectives frames protesters negatively. Alternative perspectives describing them as protesters or community members exercising their rights are absent.

parsed_output.biasedText: car attacks on ICE agents — with such incidents surging 3200% in the last year... 66 attacks compared to only two during the same period in 2024
parsed_output.explanation: While technically accurate, presenting a percentage increase from 2 to 66 as '3200%' is sensationalistic framing designed to create alarm. The absolute numbers (2 to 66) provide more meaningful context but are buried after the dramatic percentage.

parsed_output.biasedText: The shooting poured gasoline on the long-smoldering tension
parsed_output.explanation: This inflammatory metaphor frames the situation in terms of combustion and danger, using emotionally charged language rather than neutral description of increased tensions or public response.

parsed_output.biasedText: protesters who have fought back — at times violently — against the presence of agencies like ICE enforcing the law
parsed_output.explanation: This framing presents ICE as simply 'enforcing the law' without acknowledging controversies around enforcement methods or policies, while emphasizing protester violence. It omits perspectives questioning ICE practices or constitutional concerns.
